## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions — pointers
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse** (BBAAM, landed): RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming** (BBAAN/BBAAQ/BBAAR, landed): SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split** (BBAAc, landed via paddock pivot): handbook walks cover build only (BBAAf); per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests (BBAAs).
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
150	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
152	
153	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAu
154	
155	## Character
156	
157	Pure construction. Build out the `onboarding-sequence` fixture with the cases needed to produce every vessel downstream crucibles consume. No empirical run-against-fresh-state here — that's BBAAs's reslated scope.
158	
159	## Scope
160	
161	- Rename sweep: `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `onboarding-sequence` across fixture/section/manifest constants and tabtarget filenames; `onboarding-` prefix on all case names (existing five and new four).
162	- Four new cases: `enshrine-bases`, `kludge-tadmor`, `kludge-ccyolo`, `conjure-srjcl`.
163	- Expand `inscribe-reliquary` to yoke the stamp into all seven ordain-side vessels in one pass; remove per-ordain-case yoke prelude.
164	- Centralize hallmark propagation: each ordain case publishes its hallmark to a static list of consuming nameplates (sentry-tether → moriah/srjcl/pluml; airgap-bottle → moriah; plantuml-bottle → pluml; jupyter-bottle → srjcl; graft-demo → none).
165	- RCG named-string constants for vessel dirs, nameplates, case names, consumer arrays.
166	- Auto-commit pattern: kludge cases commit between sentry+bottle (clean-tree precondition); ordain cases commit at end (clean-tree for next ordain).
167	
168	## Case order
169	
170	inscribe-reliquary → enshrine-bases → kludge-tadmor → kludge-ccyolo → ordain-conjure → conjure-srjcl → ordain-airgap → ordain-bind → ordain-graft
171	
172	## Wrap criterion
173	
174	All nine cases compile; fixture invocable; single-case rerun works for each new case (mechanical confirmation, not green-against-fresh-state). Unit tests pass; no fast-suite regression.
175	
176	## Out of scope
177	
178	- Empirical run-until-green against fresh canonical state (BBAAs).
179	- moriah crucible Fixture static (still phantom; separate concern).
180	- RBS0 spec sync (BBAAq).

- **Cult-verb naming** (BBAAN/BBAAQ/BBAAR, landed): SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split** (BBAAc, landed via paddock pivot): handbook walks cover build only (BBAAf); per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests (BBAAs).

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAu

## Character

Pure construction. Build out the `onboarding-sequence` fixture with the cases needed to produce every vessel downstream crucibles consume. No empirical run-against-fresh-state here — that's BBAAs's reslated scope.

## Scope

- Rename sweep: `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `onboarding-sequence` across fixture/section/manifest constants and tabtarget filenames; `onboarding-` prefix on all case names (existing five and new four).
- Four new cases: `enshrine-bases`, `kludge-tadmor`, `kludge-ccyolo`, `conjure-srjcl`.

## Wrap criterion

All nine cases compile; fixture invocable; single-case rerun works for each new case (mechanical confirmation, not green-against-fresh-state). Unit tests pass; no fast-suite regression.

## Current pace: ₢BBAAu `onboarding-sequence-buildout` [rough]

**Character**: Pure construction. Build out the `onboarding-sequence` fixture with cases producing every vessel downstream crucibles consume. **No empirical run-against-fresh-state** — that's BBAAs's reslated scope.

**Scope** (six items):
1. **Rename sweep**: `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `onboarding-sequence` across fixture/section/manifest constants and tabtarget filenames; `onboarding-` prefix on all case names.
2. **Four new cases**: `enshrine-bases`, `kludge-tadmor`, `kludge-ccyolo`, `conjure-srjcl`.
3. **Expand `inscribe-reliquary`**: yoke stamp into all seven ordain-side vessels in one pass; remove per-ordain-case yoke prelude.
4. **Centralize hallmark propagation**: each ordain case publishes its hallmark to a static list of consuming nameplates.
5. **RCG named-string constants**: vessel dirs, nameplates, case names, consumer arrays.
6. **Auto-commit pattern**: kludge cases commit between sentry+bottle; ordain cases commit at end.

**Case order**: inscribe-reliquary → enshrine-bases → kludge-tadmor → kludge-ccyolo → ordain-conjure → conjure-srjcl → ordain-airgap → ordain-bind → ordain-graft

**Wrap criterion**: All nine cases compile; fixture invocable; single-case rerun works each case; unit tests pass; no fast-suite regression.

## Name assessment

`onboarding-sequence-buildout` — fits the docket well. "Buildout" signals pure construction; "onboarding-sequence" matches the renamed fixture. Keep.

## Proposed approach

1. **Read** current `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` + `rbtdto_onboarding.rs` + manifest entry to see existing five-case shape, then map the rename surface (constants, tabtarget filenames, manifest keys).
2. **Rename sweep first** (compile-clean checkpoint): fixture/section/manifest constants + tabtarget filenames + existing case-name `onboarding-` prefix.
3. **Mint RCG constants**: vessel dirs, nameplates, case names, consumer arrays — establish the table BEFORE writing new cases.
4. **Expand inscribe-reliquary** to all-seven-vessels yoke pass; **strip** per-ordain-case yoke prelude in same checkpoint.
5. **Add four new cases** in declared order, each landing the auto-commit pattern (kludge: sentry-then-commit-then-bottle-then-commit; ordain: end-commit).
6. **Centralize hallmark publication** into a single helper consuming the constant array per case.

## Execution strategy

**Opus, sequential.** Mechanical-but-cross-file: rename surface spans Rust constants + manifest + tabtarget filenames + section names, and the constant tables for hallmark consumers are easy to mis-thread. Sequential because steps 4 (refactor inscribe-reliquary) and 5 (add new cases) depend on the constant table from step 3 and the rename from step 2. 439	  local -r z_file="$1"

442	
443	  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate file not found: ${z_file}"
444	  test -n "${z_var_name}" || buc_die "Variable name required"

446	
447	  # Load file into array (BCG load-then-iterate)
448	  local z_lines=()
449	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
450	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
451	  done < "${z_file}"
452	
453	  # Rewrite with substitution
454	  local -r z_temp="${ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX}${z_var_name}"
455	  : > "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp file: ${z_temp}"
456	
457	  local z_found=0
458	  local z_i="" z_current=""
459	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
460	    z_current="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
461	    case "${z_current}" in
462	      "${z_var_name}"=*)
463	        z_current="${z_var_name}=${z_new_value}"

466	    esac

  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Launching Claude Code client in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" claude
}

# Run ifrit sortie adjutant inside bottle (security test dispatch)
rbob_ifrit_sortie() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Running ifrit sortie adjutant in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" \
    python3 rbtia_adjutant.py "$@"
}

######################################################################
# Drive Hallmark — rewrite a single RBRN_*_HALLMARK line in nameplate env
# Args: nameplate_file variable_name new_hallmark

zrbob_drive_hallmark() {
  local -r z_file="$1"
  local -r z_var_name="$2"
  local -r z_new_value="$3"

  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate file not found: ${z_file}"
  test -n "${z_var_name}" || buc_die "Variable name required"
  test -n "${z_new_value}" || buc_die "New hallmark value required"

  # Load file into array (BCG load-then-iterate)
  local z_lines=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_file}"

  # Rewrite with substitution
  local -r z_temp="${ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX}${z_var_name}"
  : > "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp file: ${z_temp}"

  local z_found=0
  local z_i="" z_current=""
  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
    z_current="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
    case "${z_current}" in
      "${z_var_name}"=*)
        z_current="${z_var_name}=${z_new_value}"
        z_found=1
        ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\n' "${z_current}" >> "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to write line"
  done

  test "${z_found}" = "1" || buc_die "Variable ${z_var_name} not found in ${z_file}"

  # Atomic replace
  mv "${z_temp}" "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to replace nameplate file: ${z_file}"

  buc_info "Drove ${z_var_name}=${z_new_value} → ${z_file}"
}

######################################################################
# Kludge Bottle — param1 dispatch target delegating to rbob_kludge

rbob_kludge_bottle() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  rbob_kludge
}

######################################################################
# Kludge — build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_kludge() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Delegate build to foundry kludge
  rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

######################################################################

rbob_kludge_sentry() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build sentry vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"

  # Delegate build to foundry kludge
  rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file

  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"

  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

######################################################################

rbob_ordain() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain bottle vessel via cloud build and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Ordain: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Delegate to foundry ordain (mode-dispatched: conjure, bind, or graft)
  rbfd_ordain "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from ordain output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from ordain output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Ordain installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

rbfd_ordain() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain a hallmark from a vessel (conjure, mirror, or graft based on vessel mode)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Peek at vessel mode without sourcing (sourcing makes vars readonly,
  # and the downstream function will source again via zrbfc_load_vessel)
  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  local z_mode=""
  local z_mode_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_mode_line || test -n "${z_mode_line}"; do
    case "${z_mode_line}" in
      RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=*) z_mode="${z_mode_line#RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=}"; break ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"
  z_mode="${z_mode:-conjure}"
  case "${z_mode}" in
    conjure) rbfd_build "${z_vessel_dir}" ;;
    bind)    rbfd_mirror "${z_vessel_dir}" ;;
    graft)   rbfd_graft "${z_vessel_dir}" ;;
    *)       buc_die "Unknown vessel mode: ${z_mode}" ;;
  esac

  # Chaining: read hallmark persisted by mode dispatch
  buc_step "Reading hallmark from mode dispatch output"
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark in output"

  # Metadata pipeline: graft uses combined about+vouch; conjure/bind already have about, need standalone vouch
  case "${z_mode}" in
    conjure)
      buc_info "About produced by combined conjure job — proceeding to vouch"
      rbfv_vouch "${z_vessel_dir}" "${z_hallmark}"
      ;;
    graft)
      zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit "${z_vessel_dir}" "${z_hallmark}"
      ;;
    bind)
      buc_info "About produced by combined bind job — proceeding to vouch"
      rbfv_vouch "${z_vessel_dir}" "${z_hallmark}"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown vessel mode in chaining: ${z_mode}"
      ;;
  esac
}

zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build vessel image locally for development (no Cloud Build, no GAR push)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Dirty-tree guard — kludge images must correspond to a committed state
  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
  git diff --quiet \
    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before kludge"
  git diff --cached --quiet \
    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before kludge"

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Load vessel configuration
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Kludge only supports conjure vessels (got: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE})"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE"
  test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"

```rust
fn rbtdro_kludge_nameplate(
    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
    dir: &Path,
    nameplate: &str,
    label_prefix: &str,
) -> Result<(), rbtdre_Verdict> {
    // Step 1: kludge sentry — calls rbw-cKS <nameplate>
    let sentry_result = rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, "rbw-cKS", nameplate, &[])
        .map_err(|e| rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("kludge sentry {}: {}", nameplate, e)))?;
    // ... write logs, check exit, etc.
    // The bash command rbob_kludge_sentry already drives the hallmark into the nameplate.
    // No need to re-publish via consumer-array (kludge-mode publishes to one nameplate inherently).

    // Auto-commit between sentry+bottle (clean-tree precondition for next kludge)
    rbtdro_git_commit(&format!("BBAAu kludge-{}: sentry hallmark", nameplate))?;

    // Step 2: kludge bottle — calls rbw-cKB <nameplate>
    let bottle_result = rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, "rbw-cKB", nameplate, &[])
        .map_err(...)?;
    // ... write logs, check exit ...

    // Final commit for this case
    rbtdro_git_commit(&format!("BBAAu kludge-{}: bottle hallmark", nameplate))?;

    Ok(())
}
```

Note: `rbw-cKS` and `rbw-cKB` are param1-channel tabtargets, NOT imprint-channel. Reading rbtdri_invocation.rs carefully: there are three invoke functions:
- `rbtdri_invoke` (uses ctx.fixture as imprint)
- `rbtdri_invoke_global` (no imprint)
- `rbtdri_invoke_imprint` (explicit imprint override)

For param1-channel tabtargets like `rbw-cKS tadmor`, the tabtarget itself is GLOBAL (tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh — only two dots), and the nameplate is a CLI arg. So use `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-cKS", &[nameplate], &[])`. **Verify by listing `tt/rbw-cKS.*` and `tt/rbw-cKB.*`** to confirm filename shape, then choose the right invoke function.

The `kludge-tadmor` and `kludge-ccyolo` case fns just call the helper with their nameplate name.

Probe for kludge cases: governor RBRA present (same as case 1 — kludge does need foundry credentials? Actually kludge is local-only, no GCP. Look at `rbob_kludge` to confirm. If kludge has NO governor-RBRA dependency, the probe should still verify the reliquary scratch is present — meaning case 1 ran. Pick whichever probe is genuinely load-bearing for the kludge precondition; document your choice in a code comment.)

## J. Modified case: ordain-airgap (case 7)

- Body: per existing logic (enshrine-upstream, ordain forge, enshrine-forge, ordain airgap-bottle), MINUS the per-case yokes (case 1 did them). The two `rbtdro_enshrine` calls remain.
- Hallmark propagate: airgap-bottle hallmark goes to RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_AIRGAP_BOTTLE (moriah, RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK). Forge hallmark gets captured but has no consumer (it's intermediate).
- Auto-commit: `"BBAAu ordain-airgap: airgap-bottle hallmark + propagate to moriah"`.

## K. Modified case: ordain-bind (case 8)

- Probe: governor RBRA present (existing).
- Body: ordain RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_PLANTUML (bind mode).
- Propagate hallmark to RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_PLANTUML_BOTTLE → pluml's RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK.

```rust
fn rbtdro_git_commit(message: &str) -> Result<(), rbtdre_Verdict> {
```

29	//   6. conjure_srjcl       — ordain rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter + propagate to srjcl
30	//   7. ordain_airgap       — airgap supply chain: enshrine, forge, enshrine, airgap
31	//   8. ordain_bind         — bind rbev-bottle-plantuml + propagate to pluml

122	/// reliquary stamp captured by case 1 forward to downstream cases across
123	/// per-case BURV-invocation isolation.
124	const RBTDRO_SCRATCH_RELIQUARY_REL: &str = ".rbk/.gauntlet/reliquary-stamp.txt";

127	
128	/// BURE_TWEAK signal recognized by rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild for graft mode:

130	const RBTDRO_GRAFT_TWEAK_NAME: &str = "threemodegraft";
131

134	const RBTDRO_GRAFT_SOURCE_IMAGE: &str = "busybox:latest";
135	
136	// ── Probes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
137	//
138	// Probes are pure `fn() -> Result<(), String>` per the rbtdrb_Probe shape and
139	// have no context, so they read the project root from current_dir() — theurge
140	// always launches from the project root.
141	
142	fn rbtdro_probe_root() -> Result<PathBuf, String> {
143	    std::env::current_dir().map_err(|e| format!("cannot resolve project root: {}", e))
144	}
145	
146	fn rbtdro_scratch_reliquary_path(root: &Path) -> PathBuf {
147	    root.join(RBTDRO_SCRATCH_RELIQUARY_REL)
148	}

150	/// Cases 1, 2, 8, 9 probe: governor RBRA present at the canonical secrets path.
151	/// Established by canonical-establish §2 (rbtdrk_governor_mantle).
152	fn rbtdro_probe_governor_rbra() -> Result<(), String> {
153	    let root = rbtdro_probe_root()?;
154	    let path = rbtdrk_canonical_rbra(&root, "governor")?;
155	    if !path.exists() {
156	        return Err(format!("governor RBRA absent at {}", path.display()));
157	    }
158	    Ok(())
159	}
160	
161	/// Cases 3-7 probe: reliquary stamp scratch file present and non-empty.
162	/// Established by case 1's inscribe; the scratch file lets per-vessel cases
163	/// verify case 1 ran without re-walking depot inventory.
164	/// Kludge is local-only (no GCP), so governor RBRA is not a load-bearing
165	/// precondition for kludge; the scratch presence confirms case 1 completed.
166	fn rbtdro_probe_reliquary_stamp() -> Result<(), String> {
167	    let root = rbtdro_probe_root()?;
168	    let path = rbtdro_scratch_reliquary_path(&root);
169	    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path)
170	        .map_err(|e| format!("reliquary scratch '{}' unreadable: {}", path.display(), e))?;
171	    if content.trim().is_empty() {
172	        return Err(format!("reliquary scratch '{}' is empty", path.display()));
173	    }
174	    Ok(())
175	}
